title: vismia baccifera subsp. dealbata
intro: Vismia baccifera subsp. dealbata, also known as the Amazonian sapindus tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Vismia baccifera subsp. dealbata include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
